steps.title: Use the right-triangle fact
steps.content:
A right triangle has one $90^\circ$ angle. Since all triangle angles sum to $180^\circ$, the two acute angles sum to
$$
180^\circ-90^\circ=90^\circ
$$

steps.title: Subtract the given angle
steps.content: One acute angle is $57^\circ$, so the other acute angle is $$ 90^\circ-57^\circ=33^\circ $$ The answer is B.
